meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace) for the informational site, plus a separate login-gated Annual Financial Disclosure electronic filing website
meta.netnew: FinancialDisclosureFiling — a public servant files/attests an annual financial disclosure report, today locked inside the login-only COIB filing website with no API
meta.tagline: COIB publishes its enforcement and transparency data openly, but the core compliance act — filing an annual financial disclosure — is trapped behind a login-only filing website with no API.
meta.headline: A transparency-out, attestation-in agency: 8 open datasets on the way out, but the annual disclosure filing on the way in has no machine-readable contract.

method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov/robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/coib (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) and read the Annual Disclosure pages, which describe filing 'electronically through COIB's filing website' — a login-gated electronic system with no public API. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Conflicts of Interest Board (COIB)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 8 assets with column schemas.
related_domains: nycvotes.org (Campaign Finance Board — candidate matching funds)
headline_findings: COIB is a transparency-out / attestation-in agency: its enforcement and transparency OUTPUTS are genuinely open (8 NYC Open Data datasets), but its core compliance INPUT — the annual financial disclosure filing — is locked in a login-gated filing website with no API.

The 8 open datasets cover enforcement dispositions/fines (Enforcement Fines, the most-viewed asset), donations to agencies and to elected-official-affiliated non-profits, the monthly Policymakers List, and legal defense trust donations/refunds/expenditures.

The Annual Disclosure system is the real transaction layer: 'file electronically through COIB's filing website' with a 4-week filing window each spring, but it is login-only, produces no Open Data twin, and elected officials' reports surface only as PDFs on request.
Advisory opinions (Charter Chapter 68 interpretations) and ethics-training records are published only as PDFs / behind the training site — reference corpora with no machine-readable index.

Net-new write surface: file an annual financial disclosure report (FinancialDisclosureFiling) — the single most consequential COIB resident-of-government transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' CMS for content + a separate login-gated filing website; commodity chassis, closed transaction system.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or search API on nyc.gov/site/coib.
scorecard_notes.openData: 8 real, columnar datasets published to NYC Open Data — solid transparency, but narrow (enforcement + donations + trusts), no filing data.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Enforcement dispositions are open, but the core mission data — financial disclosure filings — is not published (elected reports are PDF-on-request; others confidential).
scorecard_notes.writeApi: None — the annual disclosure filing exists only as a login-only web form.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface of any kind.
scorecard_notes.ownership: COIB owns and regularly publishes its enforcement/donation/trust data to Open Data; the filing website is a system it runs, though a black box externally.

apis_observed.note: 8 COIB datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open COIB API — enforcement dispositions, donations, policymakers, and legal defense trusts.

apis_observed.endpoint: COIB Annual Financial Disclosure filing website
apis_observed.note: 'File electronically through COIB's filing website' — the annual disclosure transaction. Login-gated, no JSON/OpenAPI, no Open Data twin; elected officials' reports posted only as PDFs on request.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API. Advisory opinions and annual reports are PDFs.
Google Maps embed on the informational site.

fruit.name: Enforcement Dispositions (Fines)
fruit.notes: Resolved conflicts-of-interest cases with fine paid to COIB / to agency / imposed-but-unpaid, other penalties, suspension days, and a narrative explanation. Keyed on Case Number. The core enforcement output.

fruit.name: Agency & Affiliated-NFP Donations
opendata_match: Donations to Not-for-Profit Organizations Affiliated with Elected Officials dx8z-6nev (12c)
Donations Received by City Agencies aqs7-v55z (5c)
Official Fundraising by City Agencies basd-2jwn (3c)
fruit.notes: Three parallel transparency datasets: gifts to agencies, official fundraising, and donations to non-profits affiliated with elected officials (Charter § 2604 / Local Law 181).

fruit.name: Policymakers List
fruit.notes: Monthly list of public servants designated policymakers (Board Rules § 1-02) — one of the roles that triggers the annual financial disclosure obligation. The bridge between the open data and the locked filing system.

fruit.name: Legal Defense Trusts
opendata_match: Legal Defense Trust Refunded Donations t3pj-3dgu (11c)
fruit.notes: Donations into, refunds out of, and expenditures from public servants' legal defense trusts (Board Rules Ch. 7). Expenditures carry the full NYC geography spine (Council District, BBL/BIN, NTA, lat/long).

fruit.name: Advisory Opinions
fruit.notes: The Board's formal interpretations of Charter Chapter 68 — published only as PDFs, no machine-readable index. A read-reference gap.

fruit.name: Ethics Training
fruit.notes: Mandatory ethics training / seminars for public servants — delivered online with no completion API or Open Data twin. A locked service-layer transaction alongside the disclosure filing.

fruit.name: Get a Waiver / Get Legal Advice
fruit.notes: Request a Charter § 2604 waiver or written legal advice — handled by web/email forms, no API. Another Siebel-style locked transaction.

fruit.name: File Annual Financial Disclosure
fruit.notes: The single most consequential COIB transaction — every senior public servant files/attests an annual financial disclosure report during a 4-week spring window. Lives only in the login-gated COIB filing website; no API, no Open Data twin. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
